perturbation.original_text: To: Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., not individually, but solely as Master Servicer on behalf of Wells Fargo Mortgage Backed Securities 2006-6 Trust ("Counterparty") Attention: Swaps Administration From: UBS AG, London Branch ("UBS AG")
perturbation.changed_text: To: Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., not individually, but solely as Master Servicer on behalf of Wells Fargo Mortgage Backed Securities 2006-6 Trust ("Beneficiary") Attention: Swaps Administration From: UBS AG, London Branch ("UBS AG")
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Counterparty' is a key term defining one of the parties in the agreement. Changing it to 'Beneficiary' introduces confusion as it is not consistently used throughout the document. While contextually, a 'Beneficiary' might be construed, the document relies solely on defining and utilizing 'Counterparty'. This change creates an in-text contradiction as all subsequent references to 'Counterparty' now become ambiguous, as if they should be instead 'Beneficiary' which impacts every clause that speaks about the obligations and rights of Counterparty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (xiv) Non-Recourse. Notwithstanding any provision herein or in the ISDA Form to the contrary, the obligations of Counterparty hereunder are limited recourse obligations of Counterparty, payable solely from the Trust Estate (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) and the proceeds thereof to satisfy Counterparty's obligations hereunder. In the event that the Trust Estate and proceeds thereof should be insufficient to satisfy all claims outstanding and following the realization of the Trust Estate and the distribution of the proceeds thereof in accordance with the Pooling and Servicing Agreement, any claims against or obligations of Counterparty under the ISDA Form or any other confirmation thereunder, still outstanding shall be extinguished and thereafter not revive.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Counterparty' and the contradicted text relies on this definition, specifically referencing the 'obligations of Counterparty.' By changing 'Counterparty' to 'Beneficiary', the changed text creates ambiguity as to whose obligations are being referred to in the contradicted section. The Non-Recourse clause specifically limits the recourse against 'Counterparty'. If 'Counterparty' is now potentially 'Beneficiary', the limitations may no longer apply to the intended party, or creates uncertainty as to which party they do apply, thus a strong contradiction exists because the change causes confusion regarding which entities are bound by the clause. The definition of misaligned terminology as using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations clearly applies.

perturbation.original_text: Fixed Rate Payer: Counterparty Fixed Amount: USD[ ] Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date: 27 April 2006
perturbation.changed_text: Fixed Rate Payer: Counterparty Fixed Amount: USD[ ] Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date: 30 April 2006
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date' from '27 April 2006' to '30 April 2006' creates a direct contradiction within the document. This creates uncertainty about the true payment date, potentially leading to disputes over late payments or breach of contract. It contradicts the initial definition, which is used for calculations and obligations elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (xii) Fully Paid Transaction. Notwithstanding the terms of Sections 5 and 6 of the Agreement, if Counterparty has satisfied all of its payment obligations under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement with respect to this Transaction, and unless UBS AG is required to return (whether pursuant to an order of a court with due authority to cause UBS AG to be required to return any such payment to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) or whether otherwise pursuant to appropriate proceedings to return to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof)) or UBS AG otherwise returns to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) upon demand of Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) any portion of such payment, then: (a) the occurrence of an event described in Section 5(a) of the Agreement with respect to Counterparty shall not constitute an Event of Default or Potential Event of Default with respect to Counterparty as the Defaulting Party in respect of this Transaction and (b) UBS AG shall be entitled to designate an Early Termination Date pursuant to Section 6 of the Agreement in respect of this Transaction only as a result of a Termination Event set forth in either Section 5(b)(i) or Section 5(b)(ii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Affected Party or Section 5(b)(iii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Burdened Party. For purposes of the Transaction to which this Confirmation relates, Counterparty's only obligation under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement is to pay the Fixed Amount on the Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date, each as defined in this Confirmation.
perturbation.justification: The change in the 'Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date' from '27 April 2006' to '30 April 2006' directly contradicts the definition of 'Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date' used in the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradicted text explicitly states: 'For purposes of the Transaction to which this Confirmation relates, Counterparty's only obligation under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement is to pay the Fixed Amount on the Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date, each as defined in this Confirmation.' Since the payment date is directly used to define obligations, changing this date creates a contradiction regarding the counterparty's payment obligations, causing uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: The parties to this Agreement hereby agree that the law of the State of New York shall govern their rights and duties in whole without regard to the conflict of law provisions thereof (other than New York General Obligations Law Sections 5-1401 and 5-1402).
perturbation.changed_text: The parties to this Agreement hereby agree that the law of the State of Delaware shall govern their rights and duties in whole without regard to the conflict of law provisions thereof (other than Delaware General Obligations Law Sections 5-1401 and 5-1402).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from 'the State of New York' to 'the State of Delaware' creates a fundamental contradiction regarding the legal framework that interprets the contract. This is particularly problematic given the numerous references to specific sections and clauses within the ISDA Form, which are likely interpreted differently under New York law versus Delaware law. By changing the law in this section, we contradict all implied reliance on New York law throughout the rest of the document. The altered reference to 'Delaware General Obligations Law Sections 5-1401 and 5-1402' is nonsensical as Delaware law would not refer to 'New York General Obligations Law'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (xvii) The ISDA Form is hereby amended as follows: for the purposes of this Transaction, the word "third" shall be replaced by he word "first" in the third line of Section 5(a)(i) of the ISDA Form; provided, however, that notwithstanding the foregoing, an Event of Default shall not occur under either if, as demonstrated to the reasonable satisfaction of the other party, (a) the failure to pay or deliver is caused by an error or omission of an administrative or operational nature; and (b) funds or the relevant instrument were available to such party to enable it to make the relevant payment or delivery when due; and (c) such relevant payment is made within the earlier of (a) three Business Days following receipt of written notice from an the other party of such failure to pay or (b) 12:00 p.m. Eastern Standard Time on the Distribution Date (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) immediately following the failure to pay.
perturbation.justification: The change in governing law from New York to Delaware creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted section specifies actions within the 'ISDA Form' using language that implies a specific legal context (potentially New York law, as originally stated). Changing the governing law to Delaware introduces legal uncertainty and renders the references to the ISDA form, particularly Section 5(a)(i), ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The parties' obligations under that section, previously under New York law, are now unclear, resulting in a clear in-text contradiction.
